title: Fimbristylis dura
taxonomy: Fimbristylis dura is classified as a species of sedge, belonging to the family Cyperaceae. It is a type of monocotyledonous plant, which means it has one seed leaf in its seedling stage. The species Fimbristylis dura is further classified under the genus Fimbristylis, a group of sedges commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, where it grows in wetland environments. Fimbristylis dura is also known by its synonyms, including Fimbristylis fasciculata and Fimbristylis cymosa.

intro: Fimbristylis dura, also known as the yellow-fruited glasswort, is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. Traditionally, Fimbristylis dura has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
